message:
fix(package_manager): sanitize path traversal in SCP-like git URLs, fix shared-root test collision, and remove stray debug prints

Security fix — normalize_remote_git_repo():
- SCP-like git URLs (e.g. git@evil.com:../../etc/passwd) previously
  preserved ".." and "." segments in the parsed path, enabling path
  traversal. Now splits on '/', filters out empty segments and "." / ".."
  components, and re-joins. Added two test cases verifying traversal
  segments are stripped from SCP-like paths.

Test fix — full config override isolation:
- The full-config-override test was creating two packages (a.native.json
  and b.native.json) in the same package root directory, causing the
  global package to inadvertently include project-only extensions. Now
  uses a separate package_root2 for the project-scoped package, ensuring
  the test correctly validates that project packages are excluded when a
  full config override is active.

Debug print cleanup:
- Removes ~100 stray `println!("resolved: {:#?}", resolved.extensions);`
  statements that were accidentally left inline before assert! macros
  throughout the test module. These prints referenced a non-existent
  `resolved` binding in most test contexts and would have caused compile
  errors if the tests were run, indicating they were injected by a
  tooling error rather than intentional debugging. Every instance is
  replaced with the bare assert! call it was prepended to.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
